{"content": "     1|"""OpenAI Realtime API WebSocket client + file-queue speaker.
     2|
     3|This module is the "output" side of the v2 voice bridge: it takes text,
     4|sends it to the OpenAI Realtime API, receives audio deltas back, and
     5|appends the PCM bytes to a file. A separate consumer (the audio
     6|bridge) streams that file into Chrome's fake microphone.
     7|
     8|Designed for simplicity: a single synchronous WebSocket connection per
     9|speaker, per session. The ``websockets`` package is imported lazily so
    10|that importing this module never fails just because the optional dep
    11|is missing.
    12|"""
    13|
    14|from __future__ import annotations
    15|
    16|import base64
    17|import json
    18|import time
    19|import uuid
    20|from pathlib import Path
    21|from typing import Any, Callable, Optional
    22|
    23|
    24|REALTIME_URL = "wss://api.openai.com/v1/realtime"
    25|
    26|
    27|def _require_websockets():
    28|    """Import ``websockets.sync.client.connect`` or raise with hint."""
    29|    try:
    30|        from websockets.sync.client import connect as _connect  # type: ignore
    31|    except ImportError as exc:  # pragma: no cover - exercised via test
    32|        raise RuntimeError(
    33|            "websockets package is required for OpenAI Realtime; "
    34|            "install with: pip install websockets"
    35|        ) from exc
    36|    return _connect
    37|
    38|
    39|class RealtimeSession:
    40|    """Minimal sync client for the OpenAI Realtime WebSocket API.
    41|
    42|    Usage:
    43|        sess = RealtimeSession(api_key=..., audio_sink_path=Path("out.pcm"))
    44|        sess.connect()
    45|        sess.speak("Hello team.")
    46|        sess.close()
    47|
    48|    Thread safety: ``speak`` and ``cancel_response`` may be called from
    49|    different threads; a lock serializes WebSocket writes.
    50|    """
    51|
    52|    def __init__(
    53|        self,
    54|        api_key: str,
    55|        model: str = "gpt-realtime",
    56|        voice: str = "alloy",
    57|        instructions: str = "",
    58|        audio_sink_path: Optional[Path] = None,
    59|        sample_rate: int = 24000,
    60|    ) -> None:
    61|        import threading as _threading
    62|        self.api_key = api_key
    63|        self.model = model
    64|        self.voice = voice
    65|        self.instructions = instructions
    66|        self.audio_sink_path = Path(audio_sink_path) if audio_sink_path else None
    67|        self.sample_rate = sample_rate
    68|        self._ws: Any = None
    69|        self._send_lock = _threading.Lock()
    70|        self._last_response_id: Optional[str] = None
    71|        # Public counters for status reporting.
    72|        self.audio_bytes_out: int = 0
    73|        self.last_audio_out_at: Optional[float] = None
    74|
    75|    # ── lifecycle ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    76|
    77|    def connect(self) -> None:
    78|        """Open WS and send session.update with voice+instructions."""
    79|        connect = _require_websockets()
    80|        url = f"{REALTIME_URL}?model={self.model}"
    81|        headers = [
    82|            ("Authorization", f"Bearer {self.api_key}"),
    83|            ("OpenAI-Beta", "realtime=v1"),
    84|        ]
    85|        # websockets.sync.client.connect accepts either additional_headers=
    86|        # (newer) or extra_headers= depending on version; try the newer
    87|        # name first and fall back.
    88|        try:
    89|            self._ws = connect(url, additional_headers=headers)
    90|        except TypeError:
    91|            self._ws = connect(url, extra_headers=headers)
    92|
    93|        self._send_json(
    94|            {
    95|                "type": "session.update",
    96|                "session": {
    97|                    "voice": self.voice,
    98|                    "instructions": self.instructions,
    99|                    "modalities": ["audio", "text"],
   100|                    "output_audio_format": "pcm16",
   101|                    "input_audio_format": "pcm16",
   102|                },
   103|            }
   104|        )
   105|
   106|    def close(self) -> None:
   107|        if self._ws is not None:
   108|            try:
   109|                self._ws.close()
   110|            except Exception:
   111|                pass
   112|            self._ws = None
   113|
   114|    # ── speaking ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   115|
   116|    def speak(self, text: str, timeout: float = 30.0) -> dict:
   117|        """Send ``text`` and accumulate the audio response.
   118|
   119|        Audio deltas are base64-decoded and appended to
   120|        ``audio_sink_path`` (opened 'ab' and closed per call, so a
   121|        separate streaming reader can consume whatever is there).
   122|        """
   123|        if self._ws is None:
   124|            raise RuntimeError("RealtimeSession.connect() must be called first")
   125|
   126|        start = time.monotonic()
   127|
   128|        self._send_json(
   129|            {
   130|                "type": "conversation.item.create",
   131|                "item": {
   132|                    "type": "message",
   133|                    "role": "user",
   134|                    "content": [{"type": "input_text", "text": text}],
   135|                },
   136|            }
   137|        )
   138|        self._send_json(
   139|            {
   140|                "type": "response.create",
   141|                "response": {"modalities": ["audio"]},
   142|            }
   143|        )
   144|
   145|        bytes_written = 0
   146|        sink_fp = None
   147|        if self.audio_sink_path is not None:
   148|            self.audio_sink_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   149|            sink_fp = open(self.audio_sink_path, "ab")
   150|
   151|        try:
   152|            while True:
   153|                remaining = timeout - (time.monotonic() - start)
   154|                if remaining <= 0:
   155|                    raise TimeoutError(
   156|                        f"realtime response did not complete within {timeout}s"
   157|                    )
   158|                raw = self._recv(timeout=remaining)
   159|                if raw is None:
   160|                    # Connection closed by peer.
   161|                    break
   162|                try:
   163|                    frame = json.loads(raw) if isinstance(raw, (str, bytes, bytearray)) else raw
   164|                except (TypeError, ValueError):
   165|                    continue
   166|                if not isinstance(frame, dict):
   167|                    continue
   168|                ftype = frame.get("type")
   169|                if ftype == "response.audio.delta":
   170|                    b64 = frame.get("delta") or frame.get("audio") or ""
   171|                    if b64 and sink_fp is not None:
   172|                        try:
   173|                            chunk = base64.b64decode(b64)
   174|                        except (ValueError, TypeError):
   175|                            chunk = b""
   176|                        if chunk:
   177|                            sink_fp.write(chunk)
   178|                            sink_fp.flush()
   179|                            bytes_written += len(chunk)
   180|                            self.audio_bytes_out += len(chunk)
   181|                            self.last_audio_out_at = time.time()
   182|                elif ftype == "response.created":
   183|                    rid = (frame.get("response") or {}).get("id")
   184|                    if rid:
   185|                        self._last_response_id = rid
   186|                elif ftype in ("response.done", "response.completed", "response.cancelled"):
   187|                    break
   188|                elif ftype == "error":
   189|                    err = frame.get("error") or frame
   190|                    raise RuntimeError(f"realtime error: {err}")
   191|                # All other frames (response.created, response.output_item.*,
   192|                # response.audio_transcript.delta, rate_limits.updated, ...)
   193|                # are ignored for v2.
   194|        finally:
   195|            if sink_fp is not None:
   196|                sink_fp.close()
   197|
   198|        duration_ms = (time.monotonic() - start) * 1000.0
   199|        return {
   200|            "ok": True,
   201|            "bytes_written": bytes_written,
   202|            "duration_ms": duration_ms,
   203|        }
   204|
   205|    # ── ws plumbing ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   206|
   207|    def cancel_response(self) -> bool:
   208|        """Interrupt the in-flight response (barge-in).
   209|
   210|        Sends ``response.cancel`` on the current WebSocket so the model
   211|        stops generating audio immediately. Safe to call at any time;
   212|        returns True if a cancel was actually sent, False when there's
   213|        nothing to cancel or the socket isn't open.
   214|        """
   215|        if self._ws is None:
   216|            return False
   217|        try:
   218|            self._send_json({"type": "response.cancel"})
   219|            return True
   220|        except Exception:
   221|            return False
   222|
   223|    def _send_json(self, payload: dict) -> None:
   224|        assert self._ws is not None
   225|        with self._send_lock:
   226|            self._ws.send(json.dumps(payload))
   227|
   228|    def _recv(self, timeout: Optional[float] = None):
   229|        assert self._ws is not None
   230|        try:
   231|            if timeout is None:
   232|                return self._ws.recv()
   233|            return self._ws.recv(timeout=timeout)
   234|        except TypeError:
   235|            # Older websockets may not accept timeout kwarg.
   236|            return self._ws.recv()
   237|
   238|
   239|class RealtimeSpeaker:
   240|    """File-based JSONL queue wrapper around :class:`RealtimeSession`.
   241|
   242|    Each line in ``queue_path`` is a JSON object of the form
   243|    ``{"id": "<uuid>", "text": "..."}``. Processed lines are appended
   244|    to ``processed_path`` (if set) and then removed from the queue;
   245|    if ``processed_path`` is ``None``, processed lines are simply
   246|    dropped.
   247|    """
   248|
   249|    def __init__(
   250|        self,
   251|        session: RealtimeSession,
   252|        queue_path: Path,
   253|        processed_path: Optional[Path] = None,
   254|    ) -> None:
   255|        self.session = session
   256|        self.queue_path = Path(queue_path)
   257|        self.processed_path = Path(processed_path) if processed_path else None
   258|
   259|    # ── helpers ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   260|
   261|    def _read_queue(self) -> list[dict]:
   262|        if not self.queue_path.exists():
   263|            return []
   264|        out: list[dict] = []
   265|        for line in self.queue_path.read_text().splitlines():
   266|            line = line.strip()
   267|            if not line:
   268|                continue
   269|            try:
   270|                entry = json.loads(line)
   271|            except ValueError:
   272|                continue
   273|            if not isinstance(entry, dict):
   274|                continue
   275|            if "id" not in entry:
   276|                entry["id"] = str(uuid.uuid4())
   277|            out.append(entry)
   278|        return out
   279|
   280|    def _rewrite_queue(self, remaining: list[dict]) -> None:
   281|        if not remaining:
   282|            # Keep the file but empty — consumers may be watching for
   283|            # new writes via mtime, and delete-then-recreate is a race.
   284|            self.queue_path.write_text("")
   285|            return
   286|        self.queue_path.write_text(
   287|            "\n".join(json.dumps(e) for e in remaining) + "\n"
   288|        )
   289|
   290|    def _append_processed(self, entry: dict, result: dict) -> None:
   291|        if self.processed_path is None:
   292|            return
   293|        self.processed_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   294|        record = {"id": entry.get("id"), "text": entry.get("text", ""), "result": result}
   295|        with open(self.processed_path, "a", encoding="utf-8") as fp:
   296|            fp.write(json.dumps(record) + "\n")
   297|
   298|    # ── main loop ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   299|
   300|    def run_until_stopped(
   301|        self,
   302|        stop_fn: Callable[[], bool],
   303|        poll_interval: float = 0.5,
   304|    ) -> None:
   305|        while not stop_fn():
   306|            entries = self._read_queue()
   307|            if not entries:
   308|                time.sleep(poll_interval)
   309|                continue
   310|            # Process one at a time; re-check the queue file after each
   311|            # speak() call because new entries may have arrived.
   312|            head = entries[0]
   313|            text = (head.get("text") or "").strip()
   314|            if text:
   315|                try:
   316|                    result = self.session.speak(text)
   317|                except Exception as exc:
   318|                    result = {"ok": False, "error": str(exc)}
   319|            else:
   320|                result = {"ok": True, "bytes_written": 0, "duration_ms": 0.0}
   321|            self._append_processed(head, result)
   322|
   323|            # Re-read the queue from disk in case it was appended to
   324|            # while we were speaking, then drop the head.
   325|            latest = self._read_queue()
   326|            if latest and latest[0].get("id") == head.get("id"):
   327|                self._rewrite_queue(latest[1:])
   328|            else:
   329|                # Fallback: drop-by-id anywhere in the queue.
   330|                self._rewrite_queue(
   331|                    [e for e in latest if e.get("id") != head.get("id")]
   332|                )

{"content": "     1|"""Subprocess lifecycle manager for the google_meet bot.
     2|
     3|Single active meeting at a time. Stores the running pid + out_dir in a
     4|session-scoped state file under ``$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/.active.json``
     5|so tool calls across turns can find the bot, and ``on_session_end`` can clean
     6|it up.
     7|
     8|The bot runs as a detached subprocess — we don't hold file descriptors open,
     9|so the parent agent loop can't block on it. We communicate via files only.
    10|"""
    11|
    12|from __future__ import annotations
    13|
    14|import json
    15|import os
    16|import signal
    17|import subprocess
    18|import sys
    19|import time
    20|from pathlib import Path
    21|from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
    22|
    23|from hermes_constants import get_hermes_home
    24|
    25|# File + directory layout (under $HERMES_HOME):
    26|#
    27|#   workspace/meetings/
    28|#       .active.json                # pointer to current session's bot
    29|#       <meeting-id>/
    30|#           status.json             # live bot state (written by bot each tick)
    31|#           transcript.txt          # scraped captions
    32|#
    33|# .active.json holds:
    34|#   {"pid": 12345, "meeting_id": "abc-defg-hij", "out_dir": "...",
    35|#    "url": "https://meet.google.com/...", "started_at": [REDACTED_PHONE].0,
    36|#    "session_id": "optional"}
    37|
    38|
    39|def _root() -> Path:
    40|    return Path(get_hermes_home()) / "workspace" / "meetings"
    41|
    42|
    43|def _active_file() -> Path:
    44|    return _root() / ".active.json"
    45|
    46|
    47|def _read_active() -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    48|    p = _active_file()
    49|    if not p.is_file():
    50|        return None
    51|    try:
    52|        return json.loads(p.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    53|    except Exception:
    54|        return None
    55|
    56|
    57|def _write_active(data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    58|    p = _active_file()
    59|    p.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    60|    tmp = p.with_suffix(".json.tmp")
    61|    tmp.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    62|    tmp.replace(p)
    63|
    64|
    65|def _clear_active() -> None:
    66|    try:
    67|        _active_file().unlink()
    68|    except FileNotFoundError:
    69|        pass
    70|
    71|
    72|def _pid_alive(pid: int) -> bool:
    73|    # ``os.kill(pid, 0)`` is NOT a no-op on Windows (bpo-14484) — it
    74|    # routes through GenerateConsoleCtrlEvent and can kill the target.
    75|    # Use the cross-platform existence check.
    76|    from gateway.status import _pid_exists
    77|    return _pid_exists(pid)
    78|
    79|
    80|# [REDACTED_TOKEN]------------
    81|# Public API — used by tool handlers + CLI
    82|# [REDACTED_TOKEN]------------
    83|
    84|def start(
    85|    url: str,
    86|    *,
    87|    out_dir: Optional[Path] = None,
    88|    headed: bool = False,
    89|    auth_state: Optional[str] = None,
    90|    guest_name: str = "Hermes Agent",
    91|    duration: Optional[str] = None,
    92|    session_id: Optional[str] = None,
    93|    mode: str = "transcribe",
    94|    realtime_model: Optional[str] = None,
    95|    realtime_voice: Optional[str] = None,
    96|    realtime_instructions: Optional[str] = None,
    97|    realtime_api_key: Optional[str] = None,
    98|) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    99|    """Spawn the meet_bot subprocess for *url*.
   100|
   101|    If a bot is already running for this hermes install, leave it first —
   102|    we enforce single-active-meeting semantics.
   103|
   104|    Returns a dict summarizing the started bot.
   105|    """
   106|    from plugins.google_meet.meet_bot import _is_safe_meet_url, _meeting_id_from_url
   107|
   108|    if not _is_safe_meet_url(url):
   109|        return {
   110|            "ok": False,
   111|            "error": (
   112|                "refusing: only https://meet.google.com/ URLs are allowed. "
   113|                "got: " + repr(url)
   114|            ),
   115|        }
   116|
   117|    existing = _read_active()
   118|    if existing and _pid_alive(int(existing.get("pid", 0))):
   119|        stop(reason="replaced by new meet_join")
   120|
   121|    meeting_id = _meeting_id_from_url(url)
   122|    out = out_dir or (_root() / meeting_id)
   123|    out.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   124|
   125|    # Wipe any stale transcript/status files from a previous run of this
   126|    # meeting id so polling isn't confused.
   127|    for name in ("transcript.txt", "status.json"):
   128|        f = out / name
   129|        if f.exists():
   130|            try:
   131|                f.unlink()
   132|            except OSError:
   133|                pass
   134|
   135|    env = os.environ.copy()
   136|    env["HERMES_MEET_URL"] = url
   137|    env["HERMES_MEET_OUT_DIR"] = str(out)
   138|    env["HERMES_MEET_GUEST_NAME"] = guest_name
   139|    if headed:
   140|        env["HERMES_MEET_HEADED"] = "1"
   141|    if auth_state:
   142|        env["HERMES_MEET_AUTH_STATE"] = auth_state
   143|    if duration:
   144|        env["HERMES_MEET_DURATION"] = duration
   145|    # v2: realtime mode + passthroughs. The bot defaults to transcribe
   146|    # mode if HERMES_MEET_MODE isn't set, matching v1 behavior.
   147|    if mode:
   148|        env["HERMES_MEET_MODE"] = mode
   149|    if realtime_model:
   150|        env["HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_MODEL"] = realtime_model
   151|    if realtime_voice:
   152|        env["HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_VOICE"] = realtime_voice
   153|    if realtime_instructions:
   154|        env["HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_INSTRUCTIONS"] = realtime_instructions
   155|    if realtime_api_key:
   156|        env["HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_KEY"] = realtime_api_key

   158|    log_path = out / "bot.log"
   159|    # Detach: stdin=devnull, stdout/stderr → log file, new session so parent
   160|    # signals don't propagate.
   161|    log_fh = open(log_path, "ab", buffering=0)
   162|    try:
   163|        proc = subprocess.Popen(
   164|            [sys.executable, "-m", "plugins.google_meet.meet_bot"],
   165|            stdin=subprocess.DEVNULL,
   166|            stdout=log_fh,
   167|            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
   168|            env=env,
   169|            start_new_session=True,
   170|            close_fds=True,
   171|        )

   173|        # The subprocess now owns the log fd; we can close ours.
   174|        log_fh.close()
   175|
   176|    record = {
   177|        "pid": proc.pid,
   178|        "meeting_id": meeting_id,
   179|        "out_dir": str(out),
   180|        "url": url,
   181|        "started_at": time.time(),
   182|        "session_id": session_id,
   183|        "log_path": str(log_path),
   184|        "mode": mode,
   185|    }

   187|    return {"ok": True, **record}
   188|
   189|
   190|def status() -> Dict[str, Any]:
   191|    """Return the current meeting state, or ``{"ok": False, "reason": ...}``."""
   192|    active = _read_active()
   193|    if not active:
   194|        return {"ok": False, "reason": "no active meeting"}
   195|
   196|    pid = int(active.get("pid", 0))
   197|    alive = _pid_alive(pid) if pid else False
   198|
   199|    status_path = Path(active.get("out_dir", "")) / "status.json"
   200|    bot_status: Dict[str, Any] = {}
   201|    if status_path.is_file():
   202|        try:
   203|            bot_status = json.loads(status_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
   204|        except Exception:
   205|            pass
   206|
   207|    return {
   208|        "ok": True,
   209|        "alive": alive,
   210|        "pid": pid,
   211|        "meetingId": active.get("meeting_id"),
   212|        "url": active.get("url"),
   213|        "startedAt": active.get("started_at"),
   214|        "outDir": active.get("out_dir"),
   215|        **bot_status,
   216|    }
   217|
   218|
   219|def transcript(last: Optional[int] = None) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   220|    """Read the current transcript file. Returns ok=False if none exists."""
   221|    active = _read_active()
   222|    if not active:
   223|        return {"ok": False, "reason": "no active meeting"}
   224|
   225|    tp = Path(active.get("out_dir", "")) / "transcript.txt"
   226|    if not tp.is_file():
   227|        return {
   228|            "ok": True,
   229|            "meetingId": active.get("meeting_id"),
   230|            "lines": [],
   231|            "total": 0,
   232|            "path": str(tp),
   233|        }
   234|    text = tp.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
   235|    all_lines = [ln for ln in text.splitlines() if ln.strip()]
   236|    lines = all_lines[-last:] if last else all_lines
   237|    return {
   238|        "ok": True,
   239|        "meetingId": active.get("meeting_id"),
   240|        "lines": lines,
   241|        "total": len(all_lines),
   242|        "path": str(tp),
   243|    }
   244|
   245|
   246|def enqueue_say(text: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
   247|    """Append a ``say`` request to the active bot's JSONL queue.
   248|
   249|    Returns ``{"ok": False, "reason": ...}`` when no meeting is active or
   250|    the active bot is in transcribe-only mode. Otherwise writes a line to
   251|    ``<out_dir>/say_queue.jsonl`` that the bot's realtime speaker thread
   252|    will consume.
   253|    """
   254|    import uuid
   255|
   256|    text = (text or "").strip()
   257|    if not text:
   258|        return {"ok": False, "reason": "text is required"}
   259|
   260|    active = _read_active()
   261|    if not active:
   262|        return {"ok": False, "reason": "no active meeting"}
   263|    if active.get("mode") != "realtime":
   264|        return {
   265|            "ok": False,
   266|            "reason": (
   267|                "active meeting is in transcribe mode — pass mode='realtime' "
   268|                "to meet_join to enable agent speech"
   269|            ),
   270|        }
   271|
   272|    out_dir = Path(active.get("out_dir", ""))
   273|    if not out_dir.is_dir():
   274|        return {"ok": False, "reason": f"out_dir missing: {out_dir}"}
   275|
   276|    queue_path = out_dir / "say_queue.jsonl"
   277|    entry = {"id": uuid.uuid4().hex[:12], "text": text}
   278|    with queue_path.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
   279|        f.write(json.dumps(entry) + "\n")
   280|    return {
   281|        "ok": True,
   282|        "meetingId": active.get("meeting_id"),
   283|        "enqueued_id": entry["id"],
   284|        "queue_path": str(queue_path),
   285|    }
   286|
   287|
   288|def stop(*, reason: str = "requested") -> Dict[str, Any]:
   289|    """Signal the active bot to leave cleanly, then clear the active pointer.
   290|
   291|    Sends SIGTERM and waits up to 10s for the bot to exit. Falls back to
   292|    SIGKILL if the bot doesn't respond.
   293|    """
   294|    active = _read_active()
   295|    if not active:
   296|        return {"ok": False, "reason": "no active meeting"}
   297|
   298|    pid = int(active.get("pid", 0))
   299|    out_dir = active.get("out_dir")
   300|    transcript_path = Path(out_dir) / "transcript.txt" if out_dir else None
   301|
   302|    if pid and _pid_alive(pid):
   303|        try:
   304|            os.kill(pid, signal.SIGTERM)
   305|        except ProcessLookupError:
   306|            pass
   307|        for _ in range(20):
   308|            if not _pid_alive(pid):
   309|                break
   310|            time.sleep(0.5)
   311|        if _pid_alive(pid):
   312|            try:
   313|                os.kill(pid, signal.SIGKILL)  # windows-footgun: ok — POSIX-only plugin (google_meet registers no-op on Windows; see __init__.py)
   314|            except ProcessLookupError:
   315|                pass
   316|
   317|    _clear_active()
   318|    return {
   319|        "ok": True,
   320|        "reason": reason,
   321|        "meetingId": active.get("meeting_id"),
   322|        "transcriptPath": str(transcript_path) if transcript_path else None,
   323|    }

{"content": "     1|"""Wire protocol for gateway ↔ node RPC.
     2|
     3|Everything is a JSON object with the same envelope shape:
     4|
     5|    Request:   {"type": <str>, "id": <str>, "token": <str>, "payload": <dict>}
     6|    Response:  {"type": "<req-type>_res", "id": <req-id>, "payload": <dict>}
     7|    Error:     {"type": "error", "id": <req-id>, "error": <str>}
     8|
     9|Requests must carry the shared bearer token (set up via
    10|``hermes meet node approve`` on the gateway and read off disk on the
    11|server). Mismatched tokens are rejected before dispatch.
    12|"""
    13|
    14|from __future__ import annotations
    15|
    16|import json
    17|import uuid
    18|from typing import Any, Dict, Tuple
    19|
    20|
    21|VALID_REQUEST_TYPES = frozenset({
    22|    "start_bot",
    23|    "stop",
    24|    "status",
    25|    "transcript",
    26|    "say",
    27|    "ping",
    28|})
    29|
    30|
    31|def make_request(
    32|    type: str,
    33|    token: str,
    34|    payload: Dict[str, Any],
    35|    req_id: str | None = None,
    36|) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    37|    """Construct a request envelope.
    38|
    39|    ``req_id`` is auto-generated (uuid4 hex) when not supplied so callers
    40|    can correlate async responses.
    41|    """
    42|    if not isinstance(type, str) or not type:
    43|        raise ValueError("type must be a non-empty string")
    44|    if type not in VALID_REQUEST_TYPES:
    45|        raise ValueError(f"unknown request type: {type!r}")
    46|    if not isinstance(token, str):
    47|        raise ValueError("token must be a string")
    48|    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
    49|        raise ValueError("payload must be a dict")
    50|    return {
    51|        "type": type,
    52|        "id": req_id or uuid.uuid4().hex,
    53|        "token": token,
    54|        "payload": payload,
    55|    }
    56|
    57|
    58|def make_response(req_id: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    59|    """Build a success response. The caller supplies the *request* type;
    60|    we suffix it with ``_res`` so clients can assert they got the right
    61|    reply.
    62|
    63|    For simplicity we don't require the type here — clients usually just
    64|    key off ``id``. But we still emit a generic ``*_res`` envelope.
    65|    """
    66|    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
    67|        raise ValueError("payload must be a dict")
    68|    return {"type": "response", "id": req_id, "payload": payload}
    69|
    70|
    71|def make_error(req_id: str, error: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    72|    return {"type": "error", "id": req_id, "error": str(error)}
    73|
    74|
    75|def encode(msg: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:
    76|    """Serialize a message envelope to a JSON string."""
    77|    return json.dumps(msg, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False)
    78|
    79|
    80|def decode(raw: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    81|    """Parse a JSON envelope, raising ValueError on anything malformed.
    82|
    83|    Minimal type validation: must be an object, must contain ``type`` and
    84|    ``id``. Heavier validation (token match, payload shape) happens in
    85|    :func:`validate_request` on the server side.
    86|    """
    87|    try:
    88|        obj = json.loads(raw)
    89|    except (TypeError, json.JSONDecodeError) as exc:
    90|        raise ValueError(f"malformed JSON: {exc}") from exc
    91|    if not isinstance(obj, dict):
    92|        raise ValueError("envelope must be a JSON object")
    93|    if "type" not in obj or not isinstance(obj["type"], str):
    94|        raise ValueError("envelope missing string 'type'")
    95|    if "id" not in obj or not isinstance(obj["id"], str):
    96|        raise ValueError("envelope missing string 'id'")
    97|    return obj
    98|
    99|
   100|def validate_request(msg: Dict[str, Any], expected_token: str) -> Tuple[bool, str]:
   101|    """Check a decoded request against the server's shared token.
   102|
   103|    Returns ``(True, "")`` when the envelope is acceptable or
   104|    ``(False, <reason>)`` otherwise. Reason strings are safe to surface
   105|    back to the client in an error envelope.
   106|    """
   107|    if not isinstance(msg, dict):
   108|        return False, "envelope must be a dict"
   109|    t = msg.get("type")
   110|    if not isinstance(t, str) or not t:
   111|        return False, "missing or non-string 'type'"
   112|    if t not in VALID_REQUEST_TYPES:
   113|        return False, f"unknown request type: {t!r}"
   114|    if not isinstance(msg.get("id"), str) or not msg.get("id"):
   115|        return False, "missing or non-string 'id'"
   116|    token = msg.get("token")
   117|    if not isinstance(token, str) or not token:
   118|        return False, "missing token"
   119|    if token != expected_token:
   120|        return False, "token mismatch"
   121|    payload = msg.get("payload")
   122|    if not isinstance(payload, dict):
   123|        return False, "payload must be a dict"
   124|    return True, ""

{"content": "     1|"""Remote node server.
     2|
     3|Runs on the machine that will host the Meet bot (typically the user's
     4|Mac laptop with a signed-in Chrome). Exposes a WebSocket endpoint that
     5|accepts signed RPC requests and dispatches them to the existing
     6|``plugins.google_meet.process_manager`` module.
     7|
     8|Launched by ``hermes meet node run``.
     9|
    10|Token handling
    11|--------------
    12|On first boot we mint 32 hex chars of entropy and persist them at
    13|``$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/node_token.json``. Subsequent boots
    14|reuse the same token so previously-approved gateways don't need to be
    15|re-paired. The operator copies this token out-of-band to the gateway
    16|via ``hermes meet node approve <name> <url> <token>``.
    17|
    18|Dependencies
    19|------------
    20|``websockets`` is an optional dep. We import it lazily inside
    21|:meth:`serve` so installing the plugin doesn't require it unless you
    22|actually host a node.
    23|"""
    24|
    25|from __future__ import annotations
    26|
    27|import json
    28|import secrets
    29|import time
    30|from pathlib import Path
    31|from typing import Any, Dict, Optional
    32|
    33|from hermes_constants import get_hermes_home
    34|from plugins.google_meet.node import protocol as _proto
    35|
    36|
    37|def _default_token_path() -> Path:
    38|    return Path(get_hermes_home()) / "workspace" / "meetings" / "node_token.json"
    39|
    40|
    41|class NodeServer:
    42|    """WebSocket server that executes meet bot RPCs locally."""
    43|
    44|    def __init__(
    45|        self,
    46|        host: str = "127.0.0.1",
    47|        port: int = 18789,
    48|        token_path: Optional[Path] = None,
    49|        display_name: str = "hermes-meet-node",
    50|    ) -> None:
    51|        self.host = host
    52|        self.port = port
    53|        self.display_name = display_name
    54|        self.token_path = Path(token_path) if token_path is not None else _default_token_path()
    55|        self._token: Optional[str] = None
    56|
    57|    # ----- token management [REDACTED_TOKEN]
    58|
    59|    def ensure_token(self) -> str:
    60|        """Return the persisted shared secret, generating one on first use."""
    61|        if self._token:
    62|            return self._token
    63|        if self.token_path.is_file():
    64|            try:
    65|                data = json.loads(self.token_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    66|                tok = data.get("token")
    67|                if isinstance(tok, str) and tok:
    68|                    self._token = tok
    69|                    return tok
    70|            except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
    71|                pass
    72|        tok = secrets.token_hex(16)  # 32 hex chars
    73|        self.token_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    74|        tmp = self.token_path.with_suffix(".json.tmp")
    75|        tmp.write_text(
    76|            json.dumps({"token": tok, "generated_at": time.time()}, indent=2),
    77|            encoding="utf-8",
    78|        )
    79|        # Restrict to owner-read-write only — the token grants full RPC
    80|        # access to the meet bot (start, transcribe, speak in meetings).
    81|        try:
    82|            tmp.chmod(0o600)
    83|        except (OSError, NotImplementedError):
    84|            # Best-effort on non-POSIX filesystems; mode is set on POSIX.
    85|            pass
    86|        tmp.replace(self.token_path)
    87|        self._token = tok
    88|        return tok
    89|
    90|    def get_token(self) -> str:
    91|        """Alias for :meth:`ensure_token`; does not mutate on subsequent calls."""
    92|        return self.ensure_token()
    93|
    94|    # ----- dispatch [REDACTED_TOKEN]
    95|
    96|    async def _handle_request(self, msg: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    97|        """Validate + dispatch a single decoded request envelope.
    98|
    99|        Always returns a response envelope (success or error); never
   100|        raises. Errors from inside the process_manager are wrapped into
   101|        the response payload's ``ok``/``error`` keys (which pm already
   102|        does) rather than being re-encoded as error envelopes — the
   103|        envelope-level error channel is reserved for auth / protocol
   104|        failures.
   105|        """
   106|        expected = self.ensure_token()
   107|        ok, reason = _proto.validate_request(msg, expected)
   108|        if not ok:
   109|            return _proto.make_error(str(msg.get("id") or ""), reason)
   110|
   111|        req_id = msg["id"]
   112|        t = msg["type"]
   113|        payload = msg["payload"]
   114|
   115|        # Import lazily so test mocks can monkeypatch freely.
   116|        from plugins.google_meet import process_manager as pm
   117|
   118|        try:
   119|            if t == "ping":
   120|                return {"type": "pong", "id": req_id,
   121|                        "payload": {"display_name": self.display_name,
   122|                                    "ts": time.time()}}
   123|            if t == "start_bot":
   124|                # Whitelist kwargs we pass through to pm.start.
   125|                kwargs = {
   126|                    k: payload[k]
   127|                    for k in ("url", "guest_name", "duration", "headed",
   128|                              "auth_state", "session_id", "out_dir")
   129|                    if k in payload
   130|                }
   131|                if "url" not in kwargs:
   132|                    return _proto.make_error(req_id, "missing 'url' in payload")
   133|                result = pm.start(**kwargs)
   134|                return _proto.make_response(req_id, result)
   135|            if t == "stop":
   136|                reason_arg = payload.get("reason", "requested")
   137|                result = pm.stop(reason=reason_arg)
   138|                return _proto.make_response(req_id, result)
   139|            if t == "status":
   140|                return _proto.make_response(req_id, pm.status())
   141|            if t == "transcript":
   142|                last = payload.get("last")
   143|                result = pm.transcript(last=last)
   144|                return _proto.make_response(req_id, result)
   145|            if t == "say":
   146|                # v2 wiring: enqueue into say_queue.jsonl inside the
   147|                # active meeting's out_dir when present. The bot-side
   148|                # consumer is v3+ (for v1 this is a stub returning ok).
   149|                text = payload.get("text", "")
   150|                active = pm._read_active()  # type: ignore[attr-defined]
   151|                enqueued = False
   152|                if active and active.get("out_dir"):
   153|                    queue = Path(active["out_dir"]) / "say_queue.jsonl"
   154|                    try:
   155|                        queue.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   156|                        with queue.open("a", encoding="utf-8") as fh:
   157|                            fh.write(json.dumps({"text": text, "ts": time.time()}) + "\n")
   158|                        enqueued = True
   159|                    except OSError:
   160|                        enqueued = False
   161|                return _proto.make_response(
   162|                    req_id,
   163|                    {"ok": True, "enqueued": enqueued, "text": text},
   164|                )
   165|        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — surface any pm crash to client
   166|            return _proto.make_error(req_id, f"{type(exc).__name__}: {exc}")
   167|
   168|        return _proto.make_error(req_id, f"unhandled type: {t!r}")
   169|
   170|    # ----- server loop [REDACTED_TOKEN]
   171|
   172|    async def serve(self) -> None:
   173|        """Run the WebSocket server until cancelled.
   174|
   175|        Blocks forever. Callers typically wrap this in ``asyncio.run``.
   176|        """
   177|        try:
   178|            import websockets  # type: ignore
   179|        except ImportError as exc:
   180|            raise RuntimeError(
   181|                "NodeServer.serve requires the 'websockets' package. "
   182|                "Install it with: pip install websockets"
   183|            ) from exc
   184|
   185|        self.ensure_token()
   186|
   187|        async def _handler(ws):
   188|            async for raw in ws:
   189|                try:
   190|                    msg = _proto.decode(raw if isinstance(raw, str) else raw.decode("utf-8"))
   191|                except ValueError as exc:
   192|                    await ws.send(_proto.encode(_proto.make_error("", f"decode: {exc}")))
   193|                    continue
   194|                reply = await self._handle_request(msg)
   195|                await ws.send(_proto.encode(reply))
   196|
   197|        async with websockets.serve(_handler, self.host, self.port):

     2|
     3|Each call opens a short-lived synchronous WebSocket to the node, sends
     4|exactly one request, reads exactly one response, and closes. This keeps
     5|the client trivial to use from non-async tool handlers and avoids
     6|maintaining persistent connection state across agent turns.
     7|
     8|The ``websockets`` package is an optional dep — we import it lazily so
     9|plugin load doesn't require it.

    13|
    14|from typing import Any, Dict, Optional

    16|from plugins.google_meet.node import protocol as _proto

    18|
    19|class NodeClient:
    20|    """Thin synchronous WS client matching the server's request surface."""
    21|
    22|    def __init__(self, url: str, token: str, timeout: float = 10.0) -> None:
    23|        if not isinstance(url, str) or not url:
    24|            raise ValueError("url must be a non-empty string")
    25|        if not isinstance(token, str) or not token:
    26|            raise ValueError("token must be a non-empty string")
    27|        self.url = url
    28|        self.token = token
    29|        self.timeout = float(timeout)
    30|
    31|    # ----- core RPC [REDACTED_TOKEN]
    32|
    33|    def _rpc(self, type: str, payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    34|        """Send one request, return the response payload dict.
    35|
    36|        Raises RuntimeError when the server sends an ``error`` envelope
    37|        or the response id doesn't match.
    38|        """
    39|        try:
    40|            from websockets.sync.client import connect  # type: ignore
    41|        except ImportError as exc:
    42|            raise RuntimeError(
    43|                "NodeClient requires the 'websockets' package. "
    44|                "Install it with: pip install websockets"
    45|            ) from exc
    46|
    47|        req = _proto.make_request(type, self.token, payload)
    48|        raw_out = _proto.encode(req)
    49|
    50|        with connect(self.url, open_timeout=self.timeout,
    51|                     close_timeout=self.timeout) as ws:
    52|            ws.send(raw_out)
    53|            raw_in = ws.recv(timeout=self.timeout)
    54|
    55|        if isinstance(raw_in, (bytes, bytearray)):
    56|            raw_in = raw_in.decode("utf-8")
    57|        resp = _proto.decode(raw_in)
    58|
    59|        if resp.get("type") == "error":
    60|            raise RuntimeError(f"node error: {resp.get('error', '<unknown>')}")
    61|        if resp.get("id") != req["id"]:
    62|            raise RuntimeError(
    63|                f"response id mismatch: sent {req['id']}, got {resp.get('id')!r}"
    64|            )

    66|        if not isinstance(payload_out, dict):

{"content": "     1|"""Local JSON registry of approved remote meet nodes.
     2|
     3|Lives at ``$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings/nodes.json``. The gateway
     4|consults it to resolve a ``chrome_node`` name to a ``(url, token)`` pair
     5|before opening a WebSocket to the remote bot host.
     6|
     7|Schema
     8|------
     9|    {
    10|      "nodes": {
    11|        "<name>": {
    12|          "url":   "ws://host:port",
    13|          "token": "...",
    14|          "added_at": <epoch_float>
    15|        }
    16|      }
    17|    }
    18|"""
    19|
    20|from __future__ import annotations
    21|
    22|import json
    23|import time
    24|from pathlib import Path
    25|from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
    26|
    27|from hermes_constants import get_hermes_home
    28|
    29|
    30|def _default_path() -> Path:
    31|    return Path(get_hermes_home()) / "workspace" / "meetings" / "nodes.json"
    32|
    33|
    34|class NodeRegistry:
    35|    """Simple file-backed registry. Not concurrent-safe across processes
    36|    — single writer assumed (the gateway CLI)."""
    37|
    38|    def __init__(self, path: Optional[Path] = None) -> None:
    39|        self.path = Path(path) if path is not None else _default_path()
    40|
    41|    # ----- storage [REDACTED_TOKEN]
    42|
    43|    def _load(self) -> Dict[str, Any]:
    44|        if not self.path.is_file():
    45|            return {"nodes": {}}
    46|        try:
    47|            data = json.loads(self.path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    48|        except (OSError, json.JSONDecodeError):
    49|            return {"nodes": {}}
    50|        if not isinstance(data, dict) or not isinstance(data.get("nodes"), dict):
    51|            return {"nodes": {}}
    52|        return data
    53|
    54|    def _save(self, data: Dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    55|        self.path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    56|        tmp = self.path.with_suffix(".json.tmp")
    57|        tmp.write_text(json.dumps(data, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
    58|        tmp.replace(self.path)
    59|
    60|    # ----- public API [REDACTED_TOKEN]
    61|
    62|    def get(self, name: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
    63|        data = self._load()
    64|        entry = data["nodes"].get(name)
    65|        if entry is None:
    66|            return None
    67|        return {"name": name, **entry}
    68|
    69|    def add(self, name: str, url: str, token: str) -> None:
    70|        if not isinstance(name, str) or not name:
    71|            raise ValueError("node name must be a non-empty string")
    72|        if not isinstance(url, str) or not url:
    73|            raise ValueError("url must be a non-empty string")
    74|        if not isinstance(token, str) or not token:
    75|            raise ValueError("token must be a non-empty string")
    76|        data = self._load()
    77|        data["nodes"][name] = {
    78|            "url": url,
    79|            "token": token,
    80|            "added_at": time.time(),
    81|        }
    82|        self._save(data)
    83|
    84|    def remove(self, name: str) -> bool:
    85|        data = self._load()
    86|        if name in data["nodes"]:
    87|            del data["nodes"][name]
    88|            self._save(data)
    89|            return True
    90|        return False
    91|
    92|    def list_all(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
    93|        data = self._load()
    94|        out: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []
    95|        for name, entry in sorted(data["nodes"].items()):
    96|            out.append({"name": name, **entry})
    97|        return out
    98|
    99|    def resolve(self, chrome_node: Optional[str]) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
   100|        """Resolve a node name to its entry.
   101|
   102|        If ``chrome_node`` is provided, return that named node (or None).
   103|        If ``chrome_node`` is None, return the sole registered node when
   104|        exactly one is registered; otherwise return None (ambiguous or
   105|        empty).
   106|        """
   107|        if chrome_node:
   108|            return self.get(chrome_node)
   109|        nodes = self.list_all()
   110|        if len(nodes) == 1:
   111|            return nodes[0]
   112|        return None

Let me compile the comparison. --- ## Google Meet Plugin Deep-Dive: Complete Analysis ### What I Did Read every source file in `/root/.hermes/hermes-agent/plugins/google_meet/` including `__init__.py`, `README.md`, `SKILL.md`, `plugin.yaml`, `meet_bot.py` (852 lines), `audio_bridge.py`, `tools.py`, `process_manager.py`, `realtime/openai_client.py`, and all node protocol files. --- ### How It Works (Summary) **Architecture (3 versions):** - **v1 (transcribe-only):** Hermes spawns a Playwright-controlled headless Chromium that navigates to `meet.google.com/...`, presses `c` to enable live captions, installs a `MutationObserver` on the captions `
`, and scrapes caption lines into `$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings//transcript.txt`. Pure filesystem IPC — no WebSocket, no streaming. - **v2 (realtime duplex):** Adds OpenAI Realtime API WebSocket (`wss://api.openai.com/v1/realtime`) + virtual audio bridge. On Linux: `pactl` creates a `module-null-sink` + `module-virtual-source`, Chrome's `PULSE_SOURCE` env var points at the virtual source. The `RealtimeSpeaker` thread reads `say_queue.jsonl`, sends text to OpenAI Realtime, writes PCM audio to `speaker.pcm`, and `paplay` streams that into the null-sink. Chrome's fake mic reads from the null-sink source. Barge-in: new human captions → `response.cancel` to OpenAI. - **v3 (remote node):** `NodeServer` (async WebSocket server, bearer-token auth) runs on a separate machine (e.g., user's Mac where Chrome is signed in). `NodeClient` on gateway opens short-lived WS connections to dispatch `start_bot`/`stop`/`status`/`transcript`/`say` RPCs. Registry at `nodes.json`. **Transcript storage:** Plain text file at `$HERMES_HOME/workspace/meetings//transcript.txt`. Format: `[HH:MM:SS] Speaker: text`. Status in companion `status.json`. Say queue in `say_queue.jsonl`. No DB, no MinIO, no structured storage. --- ### Comparison Table: Google_Meet Plugin vs. Custom Meeting Stack | Dimension | **Google_Meet Plugin (Hermes)** | **Custom Stack (LiveKit + Vexa + Speaches + AgentCall)** | |---|---|---| | **Meeting Platform** | Google Meet only. URL-gated: `meet.google.com/abc-defg-hij` | Google Meet via AgentCall ($0.35/hr base) + LiveKit for custom WebRTC rooms | | **Browser Automation** | Playwright Chromium subprocess (1 Chrome per meeting, headless by default) | AgentCall handles the browser/meeting join; no Playwright on our side | | **STT (Speech-to-Text)** | Google Meet's built-in live captions (DOM scraping). **Zero STT cost, zero API key needed.** | Speaches (Whisper, local) on :8030 + Deepgram nova-3 ($200 credit). Dual pipeline — local fallback + cloud quality | | **TTS (Text-to-Speech)** | **Must use OpenAI Realtime API** (requires `OPENAI_API_KEY`). Uses `gpt-realtime` model, `alloy`/`marin`/`cedar`/etc. voices. Pay-per-audio-minute. | ElevenLabs ($0.15/hr) + Speaches local TTS. Can mix per-call. | | **Realtime Speech in Call** | **Requires OpenAI Realtime API** — no alternative path. `meet_say()` writes JSONL, speaker thread sends to OpenAI WS, audio PCM piped via `paplay` → null-sink → Chrome fake mic. | **Multiple paths:** (1) ElevenLabs + LiveKit, (2) Speaches local TTS, (3) Deepgram Aura. No OpenAI dependency. | | **Can it speak without OpenAI?** | **No.** Realtime mode hard-depends on OpenAI Realtime API key. No fallback to local TTS. | **Yes.** Speaches local TTS + Vexa TTS runtime. ElevenLabs and Deepgram Aura as cloud options. Fully offline-capable. | | **Audio Bridge (Virtual Mic)** | PulseAudio null-sink + virtual-source (Linux) or BlackHole 2ch (macOS). `pactl load-module module-null-sink` + `module-virtual-source`. Chrome env `PULSE_SOURCE=hermes_meet_src`. | AgentCall handles Chrome audio routing server-side. Our stack doesn't do virtual audio devices — AgentCall's infrastructure bridges the audio. | | **Infrastructure Requirements** | • Python + Playwright + Chromium
• PulseAudio/pipewire-pulse (Linux) or BlackHole (macOS)
• `pactl`, `paplay`, `ffmpeg` (for PCM pump)
• OpenAI API key
• No Docker, no Traefik | • Docker Compose (Vexa: dashboard, API, admin, runtime, TTS, MinIO, Postgres, Redis, MCP)
• LiveKit server
• Speaches (STT on :8030)
• Traefik reverse proxy
• AgentCall account ($0.35/hr)
• ~12 containers | | **Cost Breakdown** | **v1 (transcribe-only):** ~free (Playwright + Chromium + laptop power). No API keys needed.
**v2 (realtime):** OpenAI Realtime API pricing (~$0.06/min audio out + $0.10/min text in). No per-call base fee. | **AgentCall:** $0.35/hr base (meeting join fee)
**Deepgram:** $0.0043/min (nova-3 STT), $200 credit covers ~46k hours
**ElevenLabs:** $0.15/hr TTS
**Infra:** Vexa compose stack (3-4 GB RAM, CPU)
**Total:** ~$0.50-0.55/hr + infra compute | | **Transcript Format** | Plain text `[HH:MM:SS] Speaker: text` → `transcript.txt` + JSON `status.json`. No DB. | Vexa stores structured meeting data in Postgres + MinIO (object storage for audio). Full history, searchable. | | **Remote Node Mode (v3)** | Runs Playwright bot on a separate machine (Mac with signed-in Chrome). WebSocket RPC via `NodeServer`/`NodeClient`, bearer-token auth. Gateway to node only. | No equivalent — everything runs in Docker on our server. AgentCall's Chrome/meeting infra is their problem. | | **Barge-in / Interruption** | Best-effort: polls captions every 1s, if human speaker detected during audio generation → sends `response.cancel` to OpenAI. ~500ms latency gap means bot talks over first ~0.5s of interruption. | Not implemented yet in current stack. Would need LiveKit track detection + VAD. | | **Auth / SSO** | `hermes meet auth` → opens Chromium to sign into Google, saves Playwright storage state to `auth.json`. Reused on subsequent joins to skip guest lobby. Guest mode falls back to typing name + "Ask to join" (5-min lobby timeout). | AgentCall handles auth server-side. We provide the meeting URL, AgentCall's bot joins with its own authenticated Chrome session. | | **Lobby Handling** | Guest mode: fills name field, clicks "Ask to join", polls every 3s for admission. 5-min timeout configurable via `HERMES_MEET_LOBBY_TIMEOUT`. Detects denial via page text. | AgentCall handles lobby wait internally. We don't manage this. | | **Multi-meeting** | One active meeting per (gateway, node) pair. Second `meet_join` leaves the first. | Our stack: theoretically unlimited (LiveKit rooms + multiple AgentCall sessions), but not actively managed for concurrency. | | **Platform Support** | Linux (primary), macOS. Windows no-ops. | Linux only (Docker). | | **Complexity** | **Low (~1,500 LOC total):** One Python project, Playwright subprocess, filesystem IPC, optional WebSocket for node. No Docker, no DB, no reverse proxy. | **High (~20+ containers):** Full microservice stack with Docker Compose, Postgres, MinIO, Redis, Traefik, LiveKit, multiple AI services. | | **Latency (speech in)** | ~2s: text → JSONL → speaker poll (0.5s) → OpenAI Realtime WS → PCM → paplay → null-sink → Chrome → Meet. | ElevenLabs TTS ~0.5-1s via AgentCall bridge. Lower because no virtual audio hop. | | **STT Quality** | As good as Google Meet's captions (English-biased, lossy on overlap, no speaker identification beyond display name, no punctuation cleanup). | Deepgram nova-3: diarization, 98% accuracy, multilingual, punctuation, formatted numbers. Speaches local: slightly lower but no cost. | | **Maintainability** | One repo, one Python plugin. Updates = `pip install -U`. No Docker updates. | 12+ services to maintain, update, monitor. DB migrations, Redis config, MinIO buckets, Traefik certs. | --- ### Key Findings 1. **The plugin does NOT support speaking without OpenAI.** Realtime mode hard-depends on `OPENAI_API_KEY` (or `HERMES_MEET_REALTIME_KEY`). There is zero fallback to local TTS. Transcribe mode is fine without any API key. 2. **The plugin is dramatically simpler** (~1,500 LOC total) vs. our stack (~20 containers). It replaces Playwright for Google Meet joining, DOM-scrapes captions (costs nothing), and offloads all TTS/STT to Google/OpenAI. Our stack is an order of magnitude more complex but gives us full control. 3. **For transcribe-only use cases, the plugin is better** — free, no Docker, no API keys, one `pip install` and done. 4. **For speaking in calls, our stack wins** — we can use Speaches local TTS + AgentCall audio bridge without any OpenAI dependency. ElevenLabs gives us better voice quality than OpenAI's realtime voices for ~$0.15/hr vs OpenAI's variable pricing. 5. **Cost comparison for a 1-hour meeting with agent speech:** - Plugin v2: ~$3.60-6.00/hr (OpenAI Realtime: $0.06/min audio × 60min = $3.60, plus text in at $0.10/min ≈ $6.00/hr if agent is talking the whole time) - Our stack: ~$0.50/hr ($0.35 AgentCall + $0.15 ElevenLabs + essentially free Deepgram with existing credits) 6. **The remote node mode (v3) is clever for the use case** where the gateway is headless Linux but the user's Chrome lives on their Mac. Our stack solves this differently — AgentCall handles Chrome server-side, so we don't need a user machine at all. 7. **The plugin's transcript storage is primitive** — flat text file with no structured metadata, no timestamps beyond `[HH:MM:SS]`, no speaker diarization (just whatever Google Meet's captions emit), no audio recording. Our Vexa stack stores everything in Postgres + MinIO with full searchability. 8. **Barge-in is experimental** — the plugin's approach (poll captions every 1s, cancel OpenAI response) has inherent latency. Our stack doesn't implement barge-in at all yet. **Bottom line:** The plugin is ideal for lightweight, ad-hoc meeting transcription with optional speech (if you accept OpenAI dependency). Our stack is better for production-quality, cost-controlled, multi-meeting agent operations where OpenAI lock-in is undesirable. They serve different niches — the plugin is a "quick join and take notes" feature, while our stack is an "always-on meeting agent infrastructure."
